title: dactylorhiza occidentalis var. kerryensis
intro: Dactylorhiza occidentalis var. kerryensis, also known as Kerry Marsh Orchid, is a rare and endangered subspecies of orchid that is native to Ireland. It is used in herbal medicine for its potential to treat various health conditions due to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, with its medicinal properties believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Herbal preparations of Dactylorhiza occidentalis var. kerryensis can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its health benefits and optimal usage.
